flashcards.q: When did Vasco da Gama arrive in India?
What was the Cartaz system?
flashcards.a: A Portuguese naval permit system for trade navigation.

flashcards.q: Name the decisive battle where an Asian power defeated the Dutch in 1741.
Who was the 'Father of modern political strategy' for the French in India?
The Battle of Plassey (1757) was fought between whom?
flashcards.a: Robert Clive (British) and Siraj-ud-daulah (Nawab of Bengal).

flashcards.q: Who was the key traitor in the Battle of Plassey?
Define the 'Subsidiary Alliance'.
flashcards.a: A treaty where Indian rulers accepted British protection and troops at their own expense.

flashcards.q: What was the 'Doctrine of Lapse'?
flashcards.a: A policy to annex princely states without a natural male heir (Lord Dalhousie).

flashcards.q: Which book by Dadabhai Naoroji exposed the economic drain of India?
flashcards.a: Poverty and Un-British Rule in India (1901).

flashcards.q: How many trillions of dollars were estimated to be drained from India (1765-1938)?
What caused the catastrophic Bengal Famine of 1770?
flashcards.a: Crop failure combined with harsh British revenue collection targets.

flashcards.q: Who gave the famous 'bones of the cotton weavers' quote in 1834?
What were the 'little republics' described by Metcalfe?
What was the goal of Macaulay's Minute on Education (1835)?
flashcards.a: To create a class of English-educated Indians ('brown Englishmen') to serve as clerks.

flashcards.q: Why were the first railways and telegraphs really built?
flashcards.a: For British military movement and resource extraction (funded by Indian taxes).

flashcards.q: Which rebellion inspired the song 'Vande Mātaram'?
Who led the Santhal Rebellion (1855-56)?
What triggered the Indigo Revolt?
flashcards.a: Forced cultivation of indigo by European planters instead of food crops.

flashcards.q: What was the religious trigger for the 1857 Rebellion?
flashcards.a: Rifle cartridges allegedly greased with cow and pig fat.

flashcards.q: Who was the titular head of the 1857 Rebellion?
Whom did British officers call the 'best and bravest of the rebels'?
Name the female rebel leader from Awadh in 1857.
Who was Nana Saheb's military adviser in 1857?
When did the British Crown take direct control of India from the Company?
What was the 'Goa Inquisition'?
flashcards.a: Religious persecution of non-Christians and converts by the Portuguese.

flashcards.q: Who was the first Indian elected to the British House of Commons?
What was 'deindustrialization' in colonial India?
flashcards.a: The ruin of Indian industries due to unfair British tariff and trade policies.

flashcards.q: Which battle checkmated the French ambitions in India?
Who translated the Bhagavad Gita into English in 1785?
What does 'un-British rule' mean in Naoroji's context?
flashcards.a: A rule that was exploitative and violated the principles of fair governance.
